sources.label: Sulzer Q1 2026 order intake media release (Apr 13, latest company release; nothing newer found)
Sulzer media relations page (no June 6-9 releases surfaced)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new evidence on the latent AI-transfer pathway (coolant quality, water reuse, process control) since June 6; today's -2.2% drift on no news leaves the un-rerated setup intact, so hold at rank 27 with a small positive delta for marginally improved asymmetry.
verdicts.bull: Flat-to-negative YTD (-1.8%) means none of the data-center fluid-handling optionality is priced in; any disclosed coolant/water-reuse design win or H2 large-project conversion (2026 guide: EBITDA margin >16.5%) rerates a $6.5B name quickly.
verdicts.neutral: Sulzer remains a diversified flow-equipment business whose AI-chain exposure is inferred, not contracted; Q1 order intake was only 'moderate' and the next hard datapoint is July 24 H1 results.
verdicts.bear: The AI-transfer thesis stays speculative while peers (Munters, Ecolab/CoolIT, Vertiv) lock up actual AI data-center cooling orders, leaving Sulzer as an energy/industrial cyclical with no AI revenue line and H2-weighted project risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 2026 results on July 24 showing large-project order conversion or any named data-center cooling/water contract.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued moderate order intake and zero disclosed AI/data-center wins, confirming the latent pathway never converts to revenue.
